page: Vultr voc-m-4c-32gb-800s-amd is an instance within the Optimized Cloud Compute family, featuring 4 vCPUs and 4 physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC Genoa processor. Operating on the x86_64 architecture at a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz, this server utilizes a shared CPU allocation model. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. Local storage is bundled with 800 GB of NVMe SSD capacity, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. No dedicated GPU hardware is present.

Benchmark results show a contrast between single-core and multi-core performance. The AMD EPYC Genoa processor delivers top-tier single-core CPU performance in stress-ng tests, placing in the top 10%. However, multi-core CPU performance falls into the average category. Memory performance is balanced, with large-block write bandwidth ranking in the top 25%, while read-only bandwidth remains average. Single-threaded compression and decompression tasks perform strongly, but multi-threaded bzip3 decompression is weak, placing in the bottom 25%.

The instance exhibits performance limitations in specific workloads. Static web serving throughput and database operations on Passmark are weak, ranking in the bottom 25%. Large language model inference is also constrained, with medium 7B model prompt processing falling into the bottom 10%. Given the high memory density of 8.0 GB per core and the 800 GB NVMe SSD storage, this server offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-intensive workloads that do not require sustained, dedicated multi-core CPU performance. It is suited for memory-optimized tasks, caching layers, and general-purpose workloads requiring moderate compute but high memory capacity.
description: The Vultr voc-m-4c-32gb-800s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute instance featuring 4 vCPUs and 4 physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC Genoa processor on the x86_64 architecture. It is configured with a shared CPU allocation, 32.0 GB of memory (8.0 GB per core), and 800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Performance benchmarks indicate top-tier single-core CPU capabilities, ranking in the top 10% in stress-ng tests, alongside strong large-block memory write bandwidth and single-threaded compression speeds. However, multi-core performance and database operations are average to weak, and static web serving throughput falls in the bottom 25%. Lacking a dedicated GPU, this memory-optimized configuration is best suited for memory-intensive applications, caching systems, and general-purpose workloads that benefit from a high memory-to-core ratio and fast local storage without requiring dedicated CPU cores.
og_description: Vultr voc-m-4c-32gb-800s-amd is a memory-optimized instance featuring 4 AMD EPYC Genoa vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and 800 GB NVMe SSD storage, delivering strong single-core performance for memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Vultr voc-m-4c-32gb-800s-amd is an AMD EPYC Genoa instance with 4 vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and 800 GB NVMe storage, optimized for memory-heavy workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized server combining AMD EPYC processing, high memory density, and fast NVMe storage for memory-intensive workloads.

32.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB per core
800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
